Notice Of Defendants Motion To Seal Defendants Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Steven R. Disharoon, Esq. of WOOD SMITH HENNING & BERMAN LLP, T:925.222.3400, for defendants/moving party;
Walt Cubberly, Esq. and Margret E. Lecocke, Esq. of WILLIAMS HART, T:713.230.2200, for plaintiff;
Jessica Dayton, Esq. of ACIKALIN DAYTON & ZOFRAS, T;650.458.2300, for plaintiff.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., RAISER, LLC'S Motion To Seal Defendants Motion To Dismiss is Granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 2.550 and 2.551 and specifies the documents to be sealed and the dates those documents were filed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Baswed On Forum Non Conveniens Brought By Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier, Llc *** Redacted Version
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Steven R. Disharoon, Esq. of WOOD SMITH HENNING & BERMAN LLP, T:925.222.3400, for defendants/moving party;
Walt Cubberly, Esq. and Margret E. Lecocke, Esq. of WILLIAMS HART, T:713.230.2200, for plaintiff;
Jessica Dayton, Esq. of ACIKALIN DAYTON & ZOFRAS, T;650.458.2300, for plaintiff.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., RAISER, LLC'S Motion To Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens, modified as discussed and stated on the record.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to dismiss the case based on forum non conveniens is granted. This action arises out of an alleged sexual assault on Plaintiff, a Texas resident represented by Texas counsel, by an Uber driver, also a Texas resident, during a ride in Texas on April 27, 2017. In April 2019, six months before filing the instant action, Plaintiff filed an action against the driver and Uber in Texas state court, but has since voluntarily dismissed ("nonsuited") that action.

[See word minutes and refer to the court reporter's transcript for the Court's full ruling.]

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
